entries.notes:
WORKING APPROACH FOR NATUURPUNT VLAAMS-BRABANT SCRAPING:

URL Structure:
- Base URL: https://www.natuurpunt.be/agenda?f%5B0%5D=province%3A187&page=X (where X is page number, 0-indexed)
- Individual event URLs contain date parameter: /agenda/event-name-id?date=DD/MM/YYYY

HTML Structure:
- Events container: div with class='views-element-container'
- Individual events: div with class='views-row'
- Event links: <a> tags with href containing '/agenda/'
- No visible pagination element on all pages, need to check multiple page numbers

Event Data Extraction:
- Date: Extract from URL parameter ?date=DD/MM/YYYY format
- Title: From link text or following h2/h3/h4 elements, needs whitespace cleanup
- Location: Parse from text using pattern "(Wandeling|Workshop|Excursie|Lezing|Activiteit)\s+([^\\n]+)" and take first word
- Time: Must fetch individual event pages and search for patterns like "14:00 tot 17:00" or "14:00"
- Default time: 10:00 AM if no time found
- Default duration: 3 hours if no end time specified

Pagination:
- Check pages 0-19 (found events on all these pages)
- Stop when 3 consecutive empty pages found
- Each page has ~20 events
- Remove duplicates by URL (some events may appear on multiple pages)

Date Filtering:
- Only include events from today onwards (skip past events)
- Dutch month abbreviations: jan, feb, mrt, apr, mei, jun, jul, aug, sep, okt, nov, dec

Technical Details:
- Use timezone Europe/Brussels
- Generate UIDs using MD5 hash of title+start+location
- Include event URL in calendar entries
- Sort events by date for better calendar organization
- Total events found: 392 unique events covering March-September 2026

Working Python Libraries:
- requests, BeautifulSoup, icalendar, pytz, datetime, re, hashlib

Success Metrics:
- Successfully found 392 events across 20 pages
- Events span from 2026-03-28 to 2026-09-13 
- Proper time extraction from individual event pages
- Generated valid .ics file (160KB) compatible with Google Calendar/Apple Calendar

WORKING APPROACH FOR NATUURPUNT VLAAMS-BRABANT SCRAPING (Updated 2026-03-28):

URL Structure:
- Base URL: https://www.natuurpunt.be/agenda?f%5B0%5D=province%3A187&page=X (where X is page number, 0-indexed)
- Individual event URLs contain date parameter: /agenda/event-name-id?date=DD/MM/YYYY

HTML Structure:
- Events container: div with class='views-element-container'
- Individual events: div with class='views-row'
- Event links: <a> tags with href containing '/agenda/' and date parameter
- CSS selector: soup.find_all('a', href=re.compile(r'/agenda/[^?]+\?date=\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}'))

Event Data Extraction:
- Date: Extract from URL parameter ?date=DD/MM/YYYY format using regex
- Title: From link text with whitespace cleanup using re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title).strip()
- Location: Parse from surrounding text or default to "Vlaams-Brabant"
- Time: Fetch individual event pages and search for patterns like "14:00 tot 17:00" or "14:00"
- Time patterns: [r'(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})\s*tot\s*(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})', r'(\d{1,2})\.(\d{2})\s*tot\s*(\d{1,2})\.(\d{2})', r'(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})', r'(\d{1,2})\.(\d{2})']
- Default time: 10:00 AM if no time found
- Default duration: 3 hours if no end time specified

Pagination:
- Check pages 0-19 (found events on multiple pages)
- Stop when 3 consecutive empty pages found
- Each page has ~20 events
- Remove duplicates by URL using dictionary with href as key

Date Filtering:
- Only include events from today onwards (event_date >= now)
- Date format: DD/MM/YYYY extracted from URL parameters

Technical Details:
- Use timezone Europe/Brussels with pytz.timezone('Europe/Brussels')
- Generate UIDs using MD5 hash of title+start+location
- Include event URL in calendar entries as description and url field
- Sort events by date for better calendar organization
- Libraries: requests, BeautifulSoup, icalendar, pytz, datetime, re, hashlib, os

Success Metrics (2026-03-28 run):
- Successfully found 333 unique events across 17 pages
- Events span from 2026-03-28 to 2026-09-13
- Generated valid .ics file (172.3 KB) compatible with Google Calendar/Apple Calendar
- Proper time extraction from individual event pages with fallback to 10:00 AM
- Timezone-aware events in Europe/Brussels timezone

Working Python code structure:
1. Initialize calendar with proper headers
2. Loop through pages 0-19 with empty page counter
3. Find event links using regex pattern on href
4. Extract date from URL, skip past events
5. Get event details and time from individual pages
6. Create calendar events with proper timezone
7. Save as .ics file

The approach is robust and handles the dynamic nature of the website well.

WORKING APPROACH FOR NATUURPUNT VLAAMS-BRABANT SCRAPING (Updated 2026-03-28):

URL Structure:
- Base URL: https://www.natuurpunt.be/agenda?f%5B0%5D=province%3A187&page=X (where X is page number, 0-indexed)
- Individual event URLs contain date parameter: /agenda/event-name-id?date=DD/MM/YYYY

HTML Structure:
- Events container: div with class='views-element-container'
- Individual events: div with class='views-row'
- Event links: <a> tags with href containing '/agenda/' and date parameter
- CSS selector: soup.find_all('a', href=re.compile(r'/agenda/[^?]+\?date=\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}'))

Event Data Extraction:
- Date: Extract from URL parameter ?date=DD/MM/YYYY format using regex
- Title: From link text with whitespace cleanup using re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title).strip()
- Location: Default to "Vlaams-Brabant" (region filter handles location)
- Time: Fetch individual event pages and search for patterns like "14:00 tot 17:00" or "14:00"
- Time patterns: [r'(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})\s*tot\s*(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})', r'(\d{1,2})\.(\d{2})\s*tot\s*(\d{1,2})\.(\d{2})', r'(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})', r'(\d{1,2})\.(\d{2})']
- Default time: 10:00 AM if no time found
- Default duration: 3 hours if no end time specified

Pagination:
- Check pages 0-19 (found events on all these pages)
- Stop when 3 consecutive empty pages found
- Each page has ~20 events
- Remove duplicates by URL using dictionary with href as key

Date Filtering:
- Only include events from today onwards (event_date.date() >= now.date())
- Date format: DD/MM/YYYY extracted from URL parameters

Rate Limiting Solution:
- Website returns HTTP 429 errors when fetching too many individual event pages quickly
- Solution: Limit detailed time extraction to first 50 events only
- Use random delays (0.5-1.5 seconds) between individual event page requests
- Add 0.5 second delay between pagination requests
- Use default 10:00 AM time for remaining events (acceptable trade-off)

Technical Details:
- Use timezone Europe/Brussels with pytz.timezone('Europe/Brussels')
- Generate UIDs using MD5 hash of title+start+location
- Include event URL in calendar entries as description and url field
- Sort events by date for better calendar organization
- Libraries: requests, BeautifulSoup, icalendar, pytz, datetime, re, hashlib, os, time, random

Success Metrics (2026-03-28 run):
- Successfully found 392 unique events across 20 pages
- Events span from 2026-03-28 to 2026-09-13
- Generated valid .ics file (232.4 KB) compatible with Google Calendar/Apple Calendar
- 50 events with detailed time extraction, 342 with default 10:00 AM time
- Timezone-aware events in Europe/Brussels timezone
- Proper handling of rate limiting with graceful degradation

Working Python code structure:
1. Initialize calendar with proper headers
2. Loop through pages 0-19 with empty page counter and small delays
3. Find event links using regex pattern on href
4. Extract date from URL, skip past events
5. Get event details and time from first 50 individual pages (with rate limiting)
6. Use default times for remaining events to avoid rate limits
7. Create calendar events with proper timezone
8. Save as .ics file

The approach is robust, handles rate limiting gracefully, and produces a high-quality calendar file with 392 events suitable for import into Google Calendar or Apple Calendar.
